data.title: QuinStreet expects 10% revenue and 20% adjusted EBITDA growth in fiscal 2026 as auto insurance demand reaccelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: QuinStreet (QNST) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Douglas Valenti highlighted, "Fiscal Q4 was another quarter of strong revenue growth and continued margin expansion. We grew total revenue 32% year-over-year and adjusted EBITDA 101%. Auto Insurance revenue grew 62% year-over-year in the quarter. Home Services revenue grew 21%." He emphasized that the year capped "a successful full fiscal year 2025, in which we grew revenue 78% to $1.1 billion and adjusted EBITDA 299% to $81 million."
  • Valenti pointed to renewed demand from auto insurance clients as key to fiscal 2025's performance and stated, "Some clients have recently begun to reaccelerate spending, and we expect strong sequential auto insurance revenue growth in the current quarter, our fiscal Q1." He noted that "auto insurance client spending continues to be generally guarded versus its potential given carrier financial strength and results and is likely to remain so until the tariff fog fully clears."
  • Valenti stated QuinStreet will "continue to invest aggressively in media capacity and products to be positioned to prosper from the pent-up and growing demand we expect in auto insurance in coming quarters and years."
  • CFO Gregory Wong reported, "For the June quarter, total revenue grew 32% year-over-year and was $262.1 million. Adjusted net income was $14.7 million, or $0.25 per share. Adjusted EBITDA grew 101% and was $22.1 million."
  • Wong added, "Our Financial Services client vertical represented 71% of Q4 revenue and grew 36% year-over-year to $186.6 million. Our Home Services client vertical represented 27% of Q4 revenue and grew 21% year-over-year to $71.7 million, another record revenue quarter for that business."
  • Wong stated, "We closed the year with $101 million of cash and equivalents and no bank debt."

Outlook

  • Valenti said, "We expect revenue in fiscal Q1 to be about $280 million and adjusted EBITDA to be about $20 million. Our initial view of full fiscal year 2026 is that revenue will grow about 10% and adjusted EBITDA will grow about 20% as we work to further expand margins."
  • Wong confirmed, "We expect full fiscal year 2026 revenue will grow about 10% and adjusted EBITDA will grow faster at about 20%. This is our initial view on fiscal 2026, and we will, of course, provide updates to our expectations as the year progresses."

Financial Results

  • Wong reported that for Q4, total revenue was $262.1 million, with adjusted net income of $14.7 million, or $0.25 per share, and adjusted EBITDA of $22.1 million.
  • The Financial Services client vertical generated $186.6 million in Q4 revenue, while Home Services generated $71.7 million. Other revenue contributed $3.8 million.
  • For the full fiscal year 2025, revenue reached $1.1 billion and adjusted EBITDA was $81.3 million. The company ended the year with $101 million in cash and no bank debt.

Q&A

  • Cal Bartyzal, Craig-Hallum Capital Group: Asked about carrier spend trends and expectations for Q1. Valenti explained that spending levels were steady early in Q4 but "began to increase as we got deeper into the quarter," with carriers indicating plans "to continue to increase those spend levels into the current quarter and have had recent indications from carriers that they also expect to increase spend further in the December quarter."
  • Bartyzal also inquired about 2026 guidance assumptions. Valenti replied, "We're giving you our early view. We are trying to be, I would say, relatively conservative versus what we might believe our full plans could represent and versus what we anticipate could happen in auto insurance."
  • Zachary Cummins, B. Riley Securities: Asked if carrier spending is broad-based. Valenti responded, "We had more carriers spending over $1 million per month with us this past quarter than we have ever had in the history of the company. So we are seeing -- so it's broad-based."
  • Cummins asked about margin expansion initiatives. Valenti outlined, "We're investing very aggressively in some new proprietary scale media opportunities... margins on those programs are only going to expand and are going to expand very strongly in coming months and quarters..." He added that operating expenses would be flat over the year due to internal restructurings and productivity improvements.
  • Patrick Sholl, Barrington Research: Inquired about tariffs' impact on Home Services. Valenti answered, "We are not seeing any indications from any of our clients that that's going to impact their spend levels or their aggression in the market."
  • Sholl also asked about the mix of media sources. Valenti said, "The acquisition we made of Aqua Vida Media... has been very successful for us."
  • Chris Sakai, Singular Research: Asked about sequential margin compression. Valenti explained, "It's a combination of media capacity still being trying to catch up with now continuing to increase auto insurance demand, Chris, and the need to keep optimizing that media against that demand."
  • Elle Niebuhr, Lake Street Capital Markets: Asked about Home Services segment growth in a lower interest rate environment. Valenti said, "We don't really model it that way. So I would guess that it would probably be supportive of more growth in Home Services because you'd likely have more home buying activity."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on carrier spending patterns, margin expansion, and the impact of tariffs, with a generally positive but probing tone as they sought clarity on growth sustainability and margin drivers.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, emphasizing strong results and a robust pipeline of initiatives. Valenti stated, "Our pipeline of growth and margin expansion initiatives is, in my view, the best, most innovative and most impactful in the history of the company."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's confidence remained high, with continued emphasis on operational execution, while analysts' questions shifted to focus more on the breadth of carrier participation and expectations for margin progression.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company delivered higher year-over-year revenue growth in Q4 (32% vs. 60% in Q3) and maintained strong margin expansion, with adjusted EBITDA rising 101% in Q4.
  • Fiscal 2025 full-year revenue growth of 78% and adjusted EBITDA growth of about 300% were noted, compared to Q3's 60% revenue growth and 145% adjusted EBITDA growth.
  • Management's tone remained confident, reiterating strong balance sheet and growth prospects. Analysts in Q4 appeared more focused on the sustainability of recent gains, margin expansion, and the impact of macro factors such as tariffs.
  • Strategic discussion in Q4 included more detail on proprietary media, margin optimization, and product development, while management continued to highlight conservative guidance for fiscal 2026.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing tariff uncertainties as a factor moderating carrier spending, but expressed confidence that the impact would be moderate based on announced agreements.
  • Valenti stated, "We do not expect a significant gap down in carrier spending from current levels, given: one, current carrier financial strength and results; two, the fact that carriers have had time to anticipate and prepare for the impact of tariffs; and three, the levels of most applicable tariff agreements announced thus far have been relatively moderate."
  • Margin compression in Q1 was attributed to investments in media capacity and the time required to optimize new media sources.
  • No major concerns were identified in Home Services regarding tariffs, and management expects continued growth in that segment.

Final Takeaway

QuinStreet delivered another record financial year, highlighted by strong revenue gains, robust adjusted EBITDA growth, and expanding cash reserves. Management is guiding to 10% revenue growth and 20% adjusted EBITDA growth in fiscal 2026, supported by broad-based carrier demand, continued investments in proprietary media and margin expansion initiatives, and new product development. While tariff uncertainties remain a factor, the company expresses confidence in its ability to navigate market dynamics, maintain operational discipline, and capitalize on long-term digital performance marketing opportunities across its verticals.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Best Buy plans to increase the workforce at its tech hub in India by 40%
data.description:
Best Buy (NYSE:BBY) plans to expand the headcount at its India tech center by over 40% in the next few months, according to Reuters. Sources indicate that the company is actively recruiting for positions related to AI, software engineering, and product management. The rapid hiring plan is in response to more global corporations setting up offices in the nation.

Best Buy (NYSE:BBY) established its first Indian technology center, known as a Global Capability Centre, in Bengaluru in 2024. The hub began by employing around 350 people, focusing on advanced work in data, artificial intelligence, product development, and digital strategy. All of Best Buy's operations in India are business-to-business or internal (digital infrastructure, product innovation, and back-end support).

The electronics retailer is lined up to report second quarter earnings on August 28. Analysts expect Best Buy (BBY) to disclose revenue of $9.23 billion and EPS of $1.22. Notably, Best Buy (BBY) has topped profit estimates in 15 out of the last 16 quarters. 

On Seeking Alpha, May Investing Ideas recently upgraded Best Buy (BBY) to a Buy rating. "The successful tariff mitigation and visible growth catalysts through the hardware refresh cycle and Switch 2 launch should drive accelerating top-line momentum, while high-margin profit streams could expand margin," read the bullish summary.

data.title: Tempus AI files mixed-securities shelf
data.description:
• Tempus AI (NASDAQ:TEM) has filed a registration statement with the SEC for a proposed sale of securities.
  • The registration statement contains three prospectuses: a base prospectus that covers the sale of mixed securities; a prospectus that covers the sale by selling stockholders of Class A common stock; and a sales agreement prospectus, which covers the sale of up to $500M of Tempus AI’s Class A common stock under a controlled equity offering.
  • TEM +7.7% premarket.

data.title: V2X announces sale of shares by Vertex Aerospace
data.description:
V2X (NYSE:VVX) announced the sale of 2 million shares of its common stock by Vertex Aerospace Holdco.

The company is not selling any shares in this offering and will not receive any proceeds. The offering is expected to close around August 11, 2025, pending standard closing conditions.

RBC Capital Markets is acting as the sole underwriter for the offering.

Subject to the closing of the offering, V2X has agreed to purchase from the underwriter 200,000 of the shares of its common stock that are subject to the offering at a price per share equal to the price to be paid to Vertex Aerospace by RBC Capital Markets.

V2X intends to fund the repurchase of its common stock with cash on hand.

Vertex Aerospace will continue to beneficially own 10,167,286 shares, or ~32.3% of V2X's outstanding common stock, after giving effect to the offering, including V2X's repurchase of shares of its common stock.

VVX -1.56% premarket.
